creator: joeb1@a1poweruser.com
text:
Its not a question of where the upgrade-info.txt lives, but a question of
informing the user of its existence and
making it accessible to the user when the conditions require it.

Now when installing the port and a older version is already installed, the
install errors out with
message saying "read /usr/ports/sysutils/qjail/upgrade-info.txt" followed by
messages saying
port is already installed. User now has access to that file.

If doing pkg_add qjail and no previous installed version, pkg_message now
contains comments to
/usr/local/share/examples/qjail/ upgrade-info.txt. User now has access to
that file after the pkg install completes.

I cannot find any way to have a pkg_add issue any comments when a older
version of the package is already installed.
This is the best work around for addressing both port and package installs
that I can come up with.


Attached is a new port_diff file.

-----Original Message-----
From: Pawel Pekala [mailto:pawelbsd@gmail.com]On Behalf Of Pawel Pekala
Sent: Friday, August 02, 2013 10:12 AM
To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org; qjail@a1poweruser.com
Subject: Re: ports/180773: [Maintainer update] sysutils/qjail Bug fix.

Any special reason why the upgrade-info.txt is not part of the distfile
package and installed with rest of the files? Right now users with
binary packages only systems are left in the dark about upgrade
instructions.
My advice would be to add this file to distfile tarball, install
upgrade-info.txt to ${DOCSDIR}, update info in Makefile, checksum in
pkg-plist so both ports and binary pkg users are on equal ground.

--
pozdrawiam / with regards
Pawe³ Pêkala
